business_rules.statement: Constructs the producer from configs/Properties (+ optional key/value serializers): builds metrics, serializers, interceptors, metadata, accumulator, and Sender, starts the I/O thread; on any failure closes partial state and rethrows as KafkaException.

Returns the producer's stable unique client instance Uuid used for telemetry correlation, blocking up to the given timeout for the broker request; throws IllegalStateException if enable.metrics.push is false and IllegalArgumentException on negative timeout.

Closes the producer, blocking until pending requests complete (or up to the given timeout, then force-closing and failing unsent records / aborting the in-flight transaction); when called from a callback degrades to a non-blocking close(0) to avoid self-join deadlock.
Resolves the compression.type config into a Compression instance, applying the configured GZIP/LZ4/ZSTD level for those codecs, else default compression for the named type.

Validates delivery.timeout.ms >= linger.ms + request.timeout.ms; throws ConfigException if the user explicitly set a smaller value, else silently raises it to that sum with a warning, returning the effective delivery timeout.

If idempotence is enabled, builds a TransactionManager from transactional.id, 2PC flag, transaction.timeout.ms and retry backoff (logging transactional vs idempotent); otherwise returns null and ignores transaction configs.
FutureFailure.get (both timed and untimed overloads) immediately throws the stored ExecutionException, surfacing the send error to the caller.
Reads linger.ms config and clamps it to Integer.MAX_VALUE, returning the batch-linger wait in milliseconds as an int.

Builds the background Sender: resolves max in-flight requests, request timeout, acks, and retries from config, creates or reuses the network client, and wires throttle metrics and the TransactionManager.

Computes the target partition for a record: uses the record's explicit partition if set, else a configured custom partitioner (rejecting negative results), else hashes the serialized key, else returns UNKNOWN_PARTITION to defer to built-in partitioning.

Returns a config copy with key/value serializer classes injected from passed instances; if an instance is null and no serializer is already configured, throws ConfigException requiring it be non-null.
Returns the optional record key used for partition selection via hashing, or null if no key was specified.
Returns the record's payload contents, which may be null.
